facts: Interesting Costa Rica Facts: Culture, History, Food & MoreCosta Rica is a stable, democratic Central American nation known for abolishing its army in 1948, protecting 25% of its land, and holding 5% of the world's biodiversity. Located between Nicaragua and Panama, its capital is San José and the population is over 5.1 million. Spanish is the official language, and the "Tico" culture is famous for the phrase "Pura Vida".
name: Costa Rica
history: Costa Rica's history is defined by its transition from an overlooked, poor Spanish colony into a stable, democratic nation known for abolishing its army in 1948. Indigenous populations thrived for thousands of years before Spanish arrival in 1502. Lacking vast mineral wealth, it developed differently from other colonies, focusing on coffee, democracy, and peace.
